tasks.title: Documentation truth and version synchronization
tasks.definition_of_done: Current-state documentation in README, opi-spec, pi-alignment-matrix, and crate READMEs identifies the workspace/crate state as 0.5.1 where it describes the current implementation, preserves historical 0.5.0 release rows, keeps English and Chinese counterparts synchronized for every changed user-facing claim, and describes opi-web-ui only as an unpublished reusable Rust component/state/rendering crate rather than a standalone browser app or pi-web-ui parity surface.

The phase4 ledger drift test (crates/opi-coding-agent/tests/phase4_ledger.rs::phase4_ledger_spec_hash_matches_current_spec) pins docs/opi-spec.md against docs/snapshots/phase4/opi-impl-state.json. For every Phase 6 user-facing documentation update, the English and Chinese counterparts carry the same current-version, package, adapter, and opi-web-ui scope claims.

session_notes.summary: Phase B gate confirmed (Proceed). Task-graph review for inferred 6.3 DoD: the DoD already enumerates the 11 protocol behaviors so it is not vague and needs no rewrite; review mapped it to concrete deliverables. Source gaps are resolved by documenting OBSERVED 0.x behavior, not changing it: (1) adapter_protocol.rs Version-Negotiation doc-comment is inaccurate (claims handshake exact-match; observed = manifest-string validation at startup via start_adapters_from_packages, and Capabilities carries no wire version field) and will be corrected; (2) unsupported-protocol diagnostic reports actual-only and will be enriched to expected+actual per the design Error Handling section. Docs: expand opi-spec.md section 10.2 to document all 11 behaviors as the honest 0.x contract, mirror in zh, add a pointer in README EN/ZH. New tests TDD red-first through the production path: adapter_rejects_unsupported_adapter_protocol (start_adapters_from_packages gate, expected-vs-actual), adapter_composes_adapters_in_deterministic_precedence_order (layer_precedence/name sort), likely adapter_lifecycle_sequence_is_deterministic (SC-5 lifecycle). Crash and structured degraded diagnostics are left to 6.4. Forbidden scope reaffirmed: no new adapter kind, no moving protocol types out of opi-coding-agent. start_commit 1d386c5, clean tree.

Interrupt-recovery: user chose to reset 6.3 to failing. Prior session completed Phase B (plan preserved in earlier session_notes) but committed no implementation and the tree is clean. Cleared start_commit and evidence bookkeeping, iteration_count->0, kept historical notes. 6.3 is again the auto-pick (deps 5.4/5.5/5.6 are passing in phase_exit[5]). A fresh Phase B gate will be re-run before implementation.

Fresh Phase B gate confirmed (Proceed, full scope) after interrupt-reset. Re-derived plan grounded in full read of adapter_protocol.rs, adapter_host.rs, adapter_extension.rs, the 4 adapter test files, harness_resource_integration.rs, spec section 10.2, and the design Workstream 3. KEY FINDING: all 11 protocol behaviors are ALREADY tested through the production path (adapter_host.rs: handshake, init/per-request timeouts, crash handshake+post-init, correlated requests, next_id, cancel best-effort, event fire-and-forget+drop-diag, state serialize/restore, shutdown reap; adapter_runtime.rs: tool/command/hook/event/state/cancel bridging + hook filtering; harness_resource_integration.rs test3/4/5: start_adapters_from_packages unsupported-protocol/kind gate + deterministic precedence order). So 6.3 is NOT from-scratch implementation. Concrete deliverables: C1 (code, TDD red-first) enrich start_adapters_from_packages diagnostics in adapter_extension.rs to expected+actual per design Error Handling line 305 (does NOT touch package_discovery.rs which is 6.4-owned and has its own diagnostic path); add focused expected+actual test in adapter_runtime.rs (6.3-owned) so harness_resource_integration.rs (6.4-owned) is not modified. C2 (docs=honest 0.x contract) fix inaccurate adapter_protocol.rs Version Negotiation doc-comment (claims wire exact-match; reality=manifest-string gate at startup, Capabilities has no version field, no runtime wire negotiation); expand spec section 10.2 EN+ZH to document all 11 behaviors as observed 0.x (not stable 1.0); README EN+ZH pointer; refresh docs/snapshots/phase4 baseline (spec hash changes). Boundaries kept: structured degraded-path diagnostics (stale lock/missing root/invalid manifest/init-timeout/process-exit/dup names/override) and harness_resource_integration.rs are 6.4's; no new wire field (honest 0.x). start_commit 1d386c5, baseline dirty set empty (clean tree).

Phase E complete. Re-derived (post-reset) scope: the 11 protocol behaviors were already tested through the production path, so 6.3 is documentation-accuracy + one diagnostic-quality fix, not from-scratch code. C1 (TDD red->green): added start_adapters_unsupported_{protocol,kind}_diagnostic_names_expected_and_actual in adapter_runtime.rs (6.3-owned, exercises start_adapters_from_packages directly; no mock binary since the gate runs pre-spawn), red on missing expected string, then enriched both start_adapters_from_packages diagnostics in adapter_extension.rs to expected+actual per design Error Handling. Did NOT touch package_discovery.rs or harness_resource_integration.rs (6.4 territory). C2: corrected adapter_protocol.rs Version Negotiation doc-comment (was inaccurate wire-handshake claim; reality = manifest-string gate, Capabilities has no version field); expanded opi-spec 10.2 EN+ZH to the honest 0.x contract covering all 11 behaviors; README EN+ZH pointer; refreshed phase4 snapshot spec-hash baseline. Independent code-reviewer APPROVED (doc claims verified against source; scope discipline clean; EN/ZH parity holds). All gates green: adapter_protocol/adapter_host/adapter_host_mock/adapter_runtime, phase4_ledger, productized_packages_docs, fmt, clippy -D warnings, doc -D warnings, smoke (full workspace test). Commit 571f89e; HEAD^=1d386c5=start_commit. Acceptance scenario phase6-adapter-protocol-contract -> met.

Reinit reconciliation (between 6.3 and 6.4, triggered at 6.4 Phase A spec-alignment gate): refreshed spec_files_sha256[docs/opi-spec.md] from 5ee01aee -> 752894910. This task's Phase E ledger write omitted the opi-implement hash refresh (it refreshed only the phase4 snapshot, keeping phase4_ledger_spec_hash_matches_current_spec green) and left the active ledger stale. A 4-agent read-only verification workflow confirmed hash-only drift: forensic attribution to 571f89e (pre=5ee01aee, post=752894910, no later commit touched the spec, diff is pure section-10.2 narrative); graph-vs-spec 6.1-6.6 unchanged; design-vs-graph a3e818f8 unchanged; phase4-consistency snapshot already at 752894910 with phase4_ledger test passing. No task-graph review gate required. 6.4 unblocked.

tasks.title: Package runtime path and degraded diagnostics hardening
tasks.definition_of_done: The package path from opi package add through declaration and lock state, later runtime package resolution, adapter startup, adapter tool or command exposure, and diagnostics is covered for a successful local-package adapter and degraded paths including stale or mismatched lock state, missing package root, invalid manifest, unsupported adapter protocol, adapter initialize timeout, adapter process exit during startup, duplicate manifest names in a precedence layer, and project package override of a global package with the same manifest name.

acceptance_scenarios.scenario: A user can add a local adapter package, restart or rebuild runtime package state, and observe the adapter tool or command available through the production package resolver and adapter startup path.

When lock state, package roots, manifests, adapter protocol, startup timeout/crash, duplicate names, or precedence overrides are degraded, doctor/startup diagnostics identify the package source, scope, manifest name when available, adapter command when relevant, expected versus actual protocol, timeout surface, and degraded runtime state.

session_notes.summary: Phase B gate confirmed (Proceed, full scope). All production edits confined to OWNED package_resolver.rs + adapter_extension.rs (adapter_host.rs NOT touched -> no task_owned_paths append, C.1a not triggered); all new tests in OWNED package_resolver.rs + harness_resource_integration.rs. TDD red->green: 7 gap tests written red-first (resolver: missing-lock-entry disabled-state, lock-drift expected/actual+disabled, missing-top-level-field through resolution, duplicate-name within precedence layer + project-over-global no-false-positive guard; adapter: unsupported-protocol/kind command+disabled, startup-failure command+disabled via SpawnFailed path), then surgical production edits: package_resolver.rs lock_missing/lock_drifted(expected+actual)/discovery_failed messages + new duplicate_name branch guarded by scope_precedence equality (fires only on same-layer collision, not project-over-global override); adapter_extension.rs protocol/kind gates + shared startup-failure arm append adapter.command + 'disabled at runtime'. adapter_host.rs NOT touched (all edits in owned package_resolver.rs/adapter_extension.rs; no task_owned_paths append, C.1a not triggered). Gap 5/6 decomposition: formatting proven via flake-free SpawnFailed path; InitializeTimeout/AdapterExited variant content (timeout value, exit code) rides through {e} and is variant-tested in adapter_host.rs (adapter_host.rs:109-119, 125-138) -- adapter_host_mock hang/crash modes exist but start_adapters_from_packages forwards no per-child env, so triggering them would pollute process env (forbidden parallel-test flake). Independent code-reviewer APPROVED (4 scrutiny points verified, no blocking issues, 2 non-blocking nits: cosmetic {:?} scope in duplicate message, out-of-scope resolver-side adapter_command_invalid already covered). Both acceptance scenarios -> met.

tasks.title: Session and RPC adapter boundary hardening
tasks.definition_of_done: Documentation clarifies opi session JSONL as a Rust-native format that represents selected pi session concepts without promising pi session v3 file compatibility, and focused tests verify extension state restoration before adapter-backed runtime behavior, extension state persistence after turns that mutate adapter state, startup diagnostics availability in RPC mode, and adapter-backed command consistency through RPC and interactive command paths where the same runtime abstraction is used.

inference_notes.reason: Draft DoD inferred from the registered Phase 6 design workstreams, testing strategy, and success criteria; requires task-graph review before execution.

Phase 6 must not implement npm, package marketplace/gallery, package update/enable/disable, package permission enforcement/sandboxing, hot reload, Node/TypeScript/jiti runtime, TypeScript extension API compatibility, pi session v3 compatibility, provider OAuth parity, pi-web-ui parity, or a new shared opi-types crate.
Dependencies inferred from Phase 6 workstream ordering and archived Phase 5 substrate tasks.
Tier inferred from touched crates and verification scope.
Commit type inferred from whether the task primarily changes documentation, tests, or runtime behavior.
Promoted to workspace tier because declared behavioral tests span opi-agent and opi-coding-agent, matching the ledger graph validation rule for cross-crate behavioral coverage.
Replaced stale runtime package helper name with the current installed-package startup entry point used by non-interactive, RPC, and interactive startup.

Part 1 edits docs/opi-spec.md (Rust-native session framing), which changes its sha256 and trips the phase4 ledger drift test (crates/opi-coding-agent/tests/phase4_ledger.rs::phase4_ledger_spec_hash_matches_current_spec). The snapshot baseline must be refreshed to the new hash, matching the pattern used by every spec-editing task (5.9, 6.1, 6.3). C.1a append performed before editing the snapshot.

acceptance_scenarios.scenario: A runtime/RPC user sees adapter startup diagnostics in RPC mode, receives adapter-backed command behavior consistently through RPC and interactive command abstractions, and has adapter extension state restored before use and persisted after mutation, while docs avoid pi session v3 compatibility claims.

evidence.verification: workspace; session_contract(15)/session_extension_state(4)/rpc_jsonl(64)/session_runtime(26) green; phase4_ledger(1) + productized_packages_docs(22) green; fmt, clippy -D warnings, doc -D warnings, smoke all green
evidence.acceptance: phase6-session-rpc-adapter-boundary; rpc_ready startup_diagnostics (red->green) + session_info resources.diagnostics tests; harness resume restore-before-adapter + persist-after-mutating-turn tests through CodingHarness resume+prompt+persist_extension_state; adapter add->list RPC consistency test through shared CodingHarness::dispatch_extension_command

session_notes.summary: Phase B gate confirmed by user ("go on" + ultracode effort). Refined gap analysis grounded in a 5-agent read-only map plus full reads of rpc.rs, harness.rs (builder/persist/restore/dispatch), runtime_packages.rs, session_extension_state.rs, session_runtime.rs, package_adapter_example.rs, and harness_resource_integration.rs. FINDINGS: (1) Docs gap (part 1) -- spec 9.3 describes the JSONL format mechanically and never calls it Rust-native; 9.4 only has negative 'why not v3' framing; no enumeration of represented vs absent pi concepts; READMEs silent. EN+ZH edit required. Editing docs/opi-spec.md changes its hash -> C.1a append docs/snapshots/phase4/opi-impl-state.json + refresh baseline (established pattern); opi-implement ledger spec hash will need reinit reconciliation after the task commit (6.3/6.4 pattern). (2) Restore-before-adapter (part 2) -- the full-harness resume->prompt->adapter-command path is ALREADY covered by harness_resource_integration.rs (6.4-owned, pre-existing Phase-5 startup-integration test), which the part-2 mapping agent missed. 6.5 adds focused coverage in its OWN gated files: session_extension_state.rs (harness-resume restore->adapter) + rpc_jsonl.rs (RPC-resume restore->adapter, genuinely uncovered). (3) Persist-after-mutating-turn (part 3) -- uncovered; add test in session_extension_state.rs. The todo example adapter advertises tools=[] (commands only), so adapter state mutates via dispatch_command(todo/add); persist_extension_state runs after the next turn (persist_turn) and writes the non-empty state -- test mutates via command dispatch then runs a turn and asserts the JSONL gained an ExtensionState entry with the item. (4) RPC diagnostics (part 4) -- diagnostics flow to resources.metadata.diagnostics and surface via session_info, but are NOT proactively emitted, and rpc.rs:6 module doc FALSELY claims 'Diagnostics go to stderr' (zero stderr writes). Decision: add an additive 'startup_diagnostics' array to the rpc_ready header (proactive, in-process testable via run_with_channels, no stderr plumbing) + test it + test session_info availability + correct the module doc to describe the real channels. Not a Phase 6 non-goal. (5) Command consistency (part 5) -- RPC dispatches via the shared CodingHarness::dispatch_extension_command (rpc.rs:668); NonInteractive/CLI have no extension-command dispatch. Add rpc_jsonl.rs test (real todo adapter: add+list through RPC) + session_runtime.rs direct-harness dispatch comparison, proving the shared abstraction behaves consistently across entry points. Production edits confined to OWNED paths: docs (opi-spec.md/zh.md, README.md/zh.md) and rpc.rs (rpc_ready field + module doc). harness.rs NOT edited (public API only) -> no C.1a for it. Forbidden scope reaffirmed: no pi session v3 compat, no npm/marketplace, no OAuth/provider parity, no pi-web-ui parity, no new opi-types crate, no moving adapter protocol types out of opi-coding-agent, no interactive CLI slash-command dispatch. start_commit 8bb0a0a, baseline dirty set empty (clean tree).

Phase E complete. Red->green TDD drove the one production code change (rpc_ready startup_diagnostics): wrote rpc_ready_header_carries_startup_diagnostics red (field absent), then added the additive startup_diagnostics array to the rpc_ready header in rpc.rs run_loop (read from harness resource metadata), correcting the module doc that falsely claimed stderr. Characterization tests (existing-working mechanisms, not red-driven code): persist-after-mutating-turn + restore-before-adapter through the real CodingHarness resume+prompt path (resume from temp JSONL, flake-free, no OPI_SESSIONS_DIR mutation); session_info diagnostics; adapter add->list consistency through RPC. Part 2 harness-resume path was ALREADY covered by harness_resource_integration.rs (6.4-owned); 6.5 adds focused coverage in its own gated session_extension_state.rs. Part 3 decomposition honest: todo example adapter advertises tools=[] so state mutates via dispatch_command and persist_extension_state captures it after the turn. harness.rs NOT edited (public API only); production edits confined to owned docs + rpc.rs. Independent code-reviewer APPROVED (5 verification areas, no blocking issues, 2 non-blocking nits). All workspace gates green: fmt, clippy -D warnings, doc -D warnings, focused + regression suites, smoke. Commit e5118fd; HEAD^=8bb0a0a=start_commit. Reinit reconciliation: refreshed spec_files_sha256[docs/opi-spec.md] 75289491 -> 9a34e870 (6.5 owns the spec edit; phase4 snapshot + phase4_ledger already refreshed/ green). Acceptance scenario phase6-session-rpc-adapter-boundary -> met.

tasks.title: Alignment guards and final Phase 6 gates
tasks.definition_of_done: Documentation guard tests reject current-phase claims for npm installation, package marketplace/gallery, package update/enable/disable, package permission enforcement or sandboxing, adapter hot reload, bundled Node.js/TypeScript/jiti runtime, custom TUI adapter components, provider streaming adapters, new first-class provider broadening, TypeScript extension API compatibility, pi session v3 compatibility, pi-web-ui parity, broad OAuth/provider parity, and moving adapter protocol types out of opi-coding-agent; positive guards keep Phase 5 MVP claims present; no Phase 6 code adds any listed non-goal; final fmt, focused tests, and clippy gates pass before phase-exit evaluation.

inference_notes.reason: Draft DoD inferred from the registered Phase 6 design workstreams, testing strategy, and success criteria; requires task-graph review before execution.

Phase 6 must not implement npm, package marketplace/gallery, package update/enable/disable, package permission enforcement/sandboxing, hot reload, Node/TypeScript/jiti runtime, TypeScript extension API compatibility, pi session v3 compatibility, provider OAuth parity, pi-web-ui parity, or a new shared opi-types crate.
Dependencies inferred from Phase 6 workstream ordering and archived Phase 5 substrate tasks.
Tier inferred from touched crates and verification scope.
Commit type inferred from whether the task primarily changes documentation, tests, or runtime behavior.
Full workspace doc gate remains a Phase F responsibility; this task owns focused guard tests plus fmt/clippy evidence needed before phase exit.
Expanded alignment guard coverage to include every Phase 6 non-goal that was previously present only in forbidden-scope notes, so the executable guard task matches the design non-goal boundary.

acceptance_scenarios.scenario: A docs edit that claims deferred ecosystem features are complete fails guard tests, while docs must still state the completed Phase 5 package CLI, local/git package sources, manifest V2 adapters, process-jsonl protocol, adapter tools/commands/hooks/events/state/cancellation/diagnostics, and trusted-code model; guards also reject bundled Node.js/TypeScript/jiti runtime, new first-class provider broadening, and adapter protocol type migration claims.
Before Phase 6 exit evaluation, formatting, focused Phase 6 tests, and clippy pass without requiring live provider credentials or user runtime data.

evidence.verification: workspace; productized_packages_docs(34) green incl. 12 new guards; cargo fmt --check --all, clippy --workspace --all-targets -D warnings, RUSTDOCFLAGS=-D warnings cargo doc --workspace --no-deps, cargo test --workspace --all-targets (every binary 0 failed), opi-impl smoke all green
evidence.acceptance: phase6-alignment-guard-coverage, phase6-final-mechanical-gates; 12 new guards (7 negative doc EN+ZH for update/enable-disable, bundled Node/TS/jiti, TS-extension-compat, pi-session-v3, pi-web-ui-parity, OAuth/provider-parity, opi-types-migration; 4 negative code-presence: no opi-types crate, no JS/TS runtime dep, 9-provider set unchanged, adapter_protocol stays in coding-agent; 1 positive capability-surface guard). Teeth: inject forbidden doc claims -> 7 RED -> revert -> GREEN (spec hash unchanged); mkdir crates/opi-types + fake provider module -> 2 code guards RED -> remove -> GREEN. Independent feature-dev:code-reviewer APPROVED (7/7 items PASS, no blocking issues). No production code changed; no docs changed.

session_notes.summary: Phase B gate confirmed by user (Proceed, full scope). Spec-alignment OK (no hash drift). Grounded in full reads of: phase6 design (Workstream 6 Alignment Guards, Non-Goals, SC 7/9/10), productized_packages_docs.rs (existing ~22 guard tests), registry.rs, root Cargo.toml, opi-ai/opi-agent src layout, and a 6-file doc-claims map (Explore). FINDING: docs already clean (every non-goal correctly deferred/rejected) -> 6.6 is additive GUARD tests, not a doc rewrite; commit is test-only (productized_packages_docs.rs), so docs/opi-spec.md hash is unchanged -> no phase4 snapshot refresh, no ledger reconciliation. Deliverables: (a) 7 negative DOC guards closing gaps EN+ZH (update/enable-disable, bundled Node/TS/jiti, TS-extension-compat, pi-session-v3-compat, pi-web-ui-parity, OAuth/provider-parity, opi-types/protocol-migration with positive-migration needles only so the legit 'Why There Is No opi-types' section is not tripped); (b) 4 negative CODE guards for SC9 (no opi-types crate, no bundled JS/TS runtime dep, first-class provider module set unchanged == 9 providers, adapter_protocol stays in coding-agent not opi-agent); (c) 1 positive guard (Phase 5 adapter capability surface). TDD teeth-test: inject a scratch block of forbidden positive claims into owned docs/opi-spec.md -> new doc guards RED -> revert exactly -> GREEN (also catches over-broad needles as false-positives on the GREEN run); code-presence teeth via mkdir crates/opi-types -> RED -> rmdir -> GREEN. Forbidden scope reaffirmed; 6.6 adds ZERO production code. start_commit e5118fd, baseline dirty set empty (clean tree).

Phase E complete. Test-only change: +361 lines in productized_packages_docs.rs adding 12 Phase 6 alignment guards. Docs were already clean (Explore agent confirmed every non-goal correctly deferred/rejected), so no doc edits and docs/opi-spec.md hash unchanged -> no phase4 snapshot refresh and no ledger reconciliation needed. TDD teeth proven two ways: (1) injected a scratch block of 7 forbidden positive claims into owned docs/opi-spec.md, ran the new doc guards -> all 7 RED with the exact injected phrases, reverted byte-identical (sha back to 9a34e870, git diff empty) -> GREEN; this also caught one over-broad needle ('TypeScript API compatibility' tripped the legit 'not a TypeScript API compatibility checklist' disclaimer because no_positive_claim does not recognize bare 'not') -> narrowed to 'TypeScript extension API'/'TypeScript-compatible'. (2) created transient crates/opi-types dir + crates/opi-ai/src/_teeth_fakeprovider.rs -> the two non-trivial code guards (workspace_has_no_opi_types_crate, first_class_provider_set_is_unchanged) RED, removed them -> GREEN; remaining 2 code guards are trivially-correct substring/file-existence assertions verified against current Cargo.toml + crate layout. All workspace gates green: fmt, clippy -D warnings, doc -D warnings, test --workspace --all-targets (0 failures), smoke. Commit d78c28f; HEAD^=e5118fd=start_commit. Both acceptance scenarios -> met. Independent feature-dev:code-reviewer APPROVED (coverage completeness/teeth/false-positive-risk/code-guard-correctness/positive-guard/scope/non-goal all PASS, 4 non-blocking nits). All 6 Phase 6 tasks now passing -> Phase F phase-exit evaluation will trigger.

1.evaluator_summary: All 18 Phase 1 tasks passing. 213 tests green. Audit fixes committed (a66465a): SSE malformed event surfacing, CRLF parsing, BashTool cross-platform, interactive TUI wiring, auth error variant, config base_url/precedence, responsive cancellation. All cross-cutting gates pass: fmt, clippy, doc, smoke.
audit_notes: Audit note (2026-05-22): tasks 1.14, 1.15, 1.16 have evaluator_required=true in ledger but Opi-Evaluator: not-required in commit footers. Root cause: evaluator gate was run but determined these tasks did not require independent evaluation beyond mechanical gates (cli-runtime tier auto-flag is conservative). Commits are not amended; footer is authoritative for audit. Ledger evidence.opi_evaluator now mirrors the actual footer value.

2.evaluator_summary: All 16 Phase 2 tasks passing. 537 tests green. All cross-cutting gates pass: fmt, clippy, doc.

3.evaluator_summary: All 13 Phase 3 tasks passing. All cross-cutting gates pass: fmt, clippy, doc. Enterprise providers (Bedrock, Azure, Vertex) reviewed by code-reviewer with bugs fixed before commit.

task_summary.title: pi-style tool selection and safety hooks

audit_notes: Phase 3 hardening (2026-05-28): Cross-audit verification of Codex/GPT-5.5/GLM-5.1 findings.
Fixed: Bedrock retry-after parsing, Azure endpoint validation, Bedrock URL image rejection, ls truncation count, compaction image placeholders, global context files, proxy wiring.
Added: --list-models CLI flag (table + NDJSON), --image non-interactive runtime, prompt_with_content agent method.
Deferred to Phase 4: Picker TUI integration, interactive --image with TUI preview.
Documented scope limitations: Bedrock full ambient credential chain (IMDS/SSO/credential_process), Vertex service-account/ADC.

4.evaluator_summary: All 19 Phase 4 tasks passing. 53 web-ui tests + 9 RPC integration tests + existing test suites all green. Cross-cutting gates pass: fmt, clippy, doc, smoke.

task_summary.title: extension trait, lifecycle hooks, custom tools, custom commands, custom messages, and extension state
web UI implementation that consumes RPC/SDK events

5.evaluator_summary: All 9 Phase 5 tasks passing. 1770 workspace tests. 15 documentation guard tests enforce truthful Phase 5 MVP descriptions. Package CLI (add/remove/list/doctor), manifest V2 with adapter declarations, process-JSONL adapter hosting via opi-extension-jsonl-v1, adapter-to-runtime bridging, and runnable example adapter packages all verified. Cross-cutting gates pass: fmt, clippy, doc, smoke.

6.evaluator_summary: All 6 Phase 6 tasks passing. Independent phase-exit evaluator (did not read the ledger) traced the design's 10 Success Criteria to code/tests: all met. (1) docs identify workspace as 0.5.1 with historical rows preserved; (2) EN/ZH docs synchronized; (3) Phase 6 baseline audit reconciles Phase 5 findings; (4) package startup success + degraded-path tests; (5) adapter protocol lifecycle/failure/cancellation/state/shutdown tests; (6) session/RPC extension-state persistence + startup-diagnostics tests; (7) documentation guards prevent overclaiming; (8) Future Ecosystem backlog exists and is non-committal; (9) no npm/marketplace/OAuth-parity/pi-web-ui-parity/permission-enforcement/TS-extension-compat/new-shared-types-crate added (all 13 non-goals confirmed-absent); (10) final gates pass (fmt, clippy -D warnings, doc -D warnings, test --workspace --all-targets 0 failures, smoke). Cross-cutting gates: fmt, clippy, doc, smoke all green.

task_summary.title: Phase 6 baseline audit and future ecosystem backlog
Package runtime path and degraded diagnostics hardening
Session and RPC adapter boundary hardening

audit_notes: Audit note (2026-06-17): Post-exit reconciliation. The 2026-06-16 exit evaluator_summary above predates independent Phase 6 audit fixes (see docs/snapshots/phase6/audit.{codex,glm5.2,opus4.6}.md and docs/superpowers/plans/2026-06-17-phase6-audit-fixes.md) landed 2026-06-17: AGENTS.md/CLAUDE.md bumped to 0.5.1 (SC1); pi-alignment-matrix.zh.md backfilled the Phase 5 row plus process-JSONL adapter content (SC2); audit-baseline.md was rewritten as a Final Reconciliation that reclassifies the stale 'open' Phase 5 findings as closed by tasks 6.3-6.6 (SC3); and CodingHarness::dispatch_extension_command now restores and persists extension state so command-only RPC sessions survive restart (SC6/H8). The SC2 and SC3 deliverables are now substantively correct; the 2026-06-16 summary should be read in this reconciliation context.

Deferred (non-blocking, per the three Phase 6 audits): H4 init-timeout/process-exit variant content is still asserted via host-layer wildcards rather than driven through start_adapters_from_packages; M1 diagnostic rows still emit adapter_command: null; M4 the opi-spec 9.3 entry-type table still lists types the 4-variant SessionEntry enum does not persist (Phase 11); M9 the live ledger spec-hash has no commit-time gate; N1 was the missing CHANGELOG [0.5.1] link (fixed 2026-06-17). Graceful production teardown of installed adapter packages remains best-effort kill-on-drop by documented contract.
